title: aria-label & friends
description: When HTML alone can't say enough.

questions.prompt: You have an icon-only button. Screen readers see nothing. What do you add?
questions.explanation: aria-label gives an accessible name to elements with no visible text.

questions.prompt: Which is the FIRST rule of ARIA?
questions.explanation: No ARIA is better than bad ARIA. Use the right native element first.
aria-label="Close" gives the button a name for assistive tech.

options: labelledby refers to another element's id for the label
questions.explanation: labelledby is great when the label already exists elsewhere on the page.

options: Removes it from the a11y tree (screen readers skip)
questions.explanation: Purely for AT. Visual users still see it.
status = polite. alert = assertive (interrupts). Pick per urgency.

questions.prompt: Which is the CORRECT way to hide something ONLY visually?
options: A .sr-only class with clip/positioning that keeps it in a11y tree
questions.explanation: Screen-reader-only content should be off-screen but not display:none, which removes from AT.

questions.prompt: Which is the tab order that matches the visual layout?
options: Source order — unless tabindex breaks it
questions.explanation: Default: DOM source order. Avoid positive tabindex which reorders unpredictably.
